description: In a coffee shop, Rachel, wearing a black and white top with a half-apron, stands facing Ross, who is holding a baby. Rachel gestures with one hand and says, "I am going to go pour these very nice people some coffee."
Ross says, "Okay."
Rachel extends one hand outward, looks toward the counter, then brings her hands back. She says, "Oh, look at that. I don't have a pot. I don't have a pot."
Rachel steps sideways, pointing with one hand, then walks quickly past Ross toward the door. She says, "Well, hey, maybe I've got one at home. Or in scarsdale. Hey, is that a door?"
Rachel walks out through the glass door of the coffee shop as audience laughter is heard.

The setting changes to the exterior of a brick apartment building. The setting then changes to an apartment living room. Phoebe opens the front door and walks in, wearing a blue leather jacket and carrying a backpack.
Richard sits in an armchair holding an open newspaper. He looks toward the door and says, "Hey, Phoebes."
Richard asks, "What's happening?" as Phoebe walks past him. Phoebe replies, "Oh, okay."
Phoebe stops and faces Richard. "Murder, cancer, soccer teams eating each other in the Andes." Audience laughter sounds.
Richard stares up at Phoebe.
Richard says, "I was really just making small talk." Phoebe replies, "Okay."
Phoebe says, "Well, I was just venting." Monica, wearing a red v-neck top, sits on the floor behind a coffee table and says, "They watched the huh? What is happening to the"
Phoebe looks downward, shaking her head.
Phoebe says, "no, no."
Phoebe continues, "Because E.T."
Phoebe says, "leaves and Rocky loses. Charlotte dies." Richard looks up and asks, "Charlotte who?" Phoebe turns her head slightly and says, "With the web. The spider."
Richard asks, "She dies?" Phoebe nods and says, "She does. She has"
Phoebe continues, "babies and"
Phoebe looks down briefly, then raises her head.
Phoebe says, "You know, 'Hey. Welcome home from the hospital.' Thud," followed by audience laughter.
Monica looks up at Phoebe. Richard remains seated holding the newspaper.
Monica stands up from the floor and asks, "You want to feel better?"
Phoebe replies, "Yeah." Monica says, "Okay,"
Monica walks to a wooden cabinet beneath a television and opens one of its doors.
Monica retrieves a VHS tape, turns, and hands it to Phoebe. Monica says, "here, watch this." Phoebe takes the tape, looks at it, and says, "It's a Wonderful Life."
Phoebe says, "Yes, I've heard of this." Monica says, "So you can't lose. It's there in the"
Monica gestures with her hands as she finishes, "title. Wonderfulness is baked right in."
Phoebe holds up the tape. "Please. I almost fell for that with Pride of the Yankees. I thought I was going to see a film about"
Phoebe continues, "Yankee pride. And then, boom. The guy gets Lou Gehrig's disease." Audience laughter plays.
Richard closes his eyes briefly, then tilts his head slightly toward Phoebe.
Richard raises one hand with his index finger extended. "The guy was Lou Gehrig? Didn't you kind of see it coming?" Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe lowers her gaze. Monica turns toward Phoebe and extends one hand.
Monica says, "Pheebs, just watch that. I promise you it'll restore all your faith in humanity."
Chandler walks through the front door wearing a black t-shirt. He rubs his hands together and says, "Hey, big guy."
Chandler continues, "Game time." Richard points his finger and says, "Hey. Be right there." Monica turns to Chandler and asks, "There's a game?"
Chandler steps further into the room, maintaining his hand-rubbing motion. "Yeah, I just got my pickup sticks back from the shop," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler points his finger outward.
Chandler says, "Bring your nerves of steel." He then turns and walks back out the door.
Richard folds his newspaper and places it on the coffee table.
Richard places his hands on his knees and says, "It's the basketball playoffs."
Richard pushes himself up from the armchair.
Monica walks toward Richard, gesturing with her hands. "Honey, I appreciate this, but you don't have to keep hanging out with them for me. I mean, they have each other."
Richard stops, facing Monica. "Oh, no, honey. I mean, don't worry. I like hanging out with those guys."
Richard gestures with both hands.
Richard says, "It's fun for me. They're different than my other friends."
Richard continues, "They don't start sentences with, you know who just died shoveling snow?" Audience laughter sounds.
Monica nods slightly.
Monica says, "All right, that's great."
Richard turns and walks toward the front door.
Monica says, "Then just go Knicks."
Richard stops near the door, turns back, and says, "It's the college playoffs." Monica says, "Oh. Then go, vassar." Audience laughter plays.
Richard raises one hand and says, "They're not in it." Monica replies, "Okay, then just go." Richard says, "Okay," and opens the door.
Richard exits the apartment. Monica stands near the closed door and places one hand against it, then turns away.
Monica walks toward the kitchen area, raising her hands. "Why does this bother me so much? I mean, I don't want to be one of those people who tells their boyfriend they want to spend"
Monica continues speaking while standing near the kitchen counter. "24 hours a day with them."
Phoebe stands nearby holding a water bottle. She says, "Sure." Monica says, "It's just that he doesn't have that much free time, and I don't know, what do I do?"
Phoebe replies, "Does it matter? You're ultimately just going to"
Phoebe continues, "die or get divorced or have to blow your pet's head off," accompanied by audience laughter.
Rachel quickly enters the apartment through the front door, wearing the same outfit as before. Monica turns to look at her.
Rachel walks past Phoebe and Monica, saying, "Me too. I got a question. Richard made plans again with the guys."
Monica turns her body toward Rachel and gestures with one hand. "Yeah, well, Ross just made plans for the whole"
Monica finishes, "century." Rachel stops, turning back toward Monica. "You know what? I think I'm gonna go to my room and read Cosmo. Maybe there's something helpful in there."
Rachel turns and walks rapidly toward the hallway. Monica points a finger in Rachel's direction.
Monica says, "Not. At least maybe I can learn how to do an at home bikini wax with leftover Christmas candles," followed by audience laughter.
Monica lowers her hand and turns her head slightly.
In an apartment, Ross walks through the front door carrying a baby in his arms. The door has a yellow peephole frame.

Ross sets the baby down off-screen. "Okay, what the hell happened back there?" he asks. Rachel, wearing a black and white short-sleeved top and a black skirt, stands in the kitchen area with her arms outstretched. "I don't know. You tell me," Rachel says.
Rachel drops her hands to her sides, then raises them slightly.
Rachel continues, "1 minute, I'm holding Ben like a football."

Rachel gestures with both hands. "The next thing I know, I've got two kids, I'm living in Scarsdale complaining about the taxes." Ross stands facing her, wearing a maroon sweater with a backpack strap over his shoulder.
Ross slightly tilts his head.
Ross raises his hand, gesturing as he speaks. "Well, I'm sorry. I think about stuff, you know?"
Ross gestures again. "I mean, you're at work, you're assembling bones, your mind wanders." Rachel raises her hands toward her head. "Ross, you have"
Rachel continues, "planned out the next 20 years of our lives. We've been dating for six weeks." Ross walks further into the room.
Ross asks, "Oh, come on. What? You never think about our future?" Rachel replies, "Yes,"
Rachel opens the refrigerator door and looks inside. "but I think about whose apartment we're going to sleep at tomorrow night and where we're going to have dinner next"
Rachel turns back toward Ross.
Rachel closes the refrigerator, steps forward, and gestures. "Saturday night. I do not think about what our children's names are going to be."
Ross puts his hands on his hips.
Rachel points a finger at Ross. "You know, what our children's names are going to"
Audience laughter is audible. Ross looks down, then raises his hands in a questioning gesture.
Ross speaks with his hands raised. "mean. You know, I read a book, and there was a girl named Emily."
Ross lowers his hands. "And I thought that might be good."
Rachel holds a small jar. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel asks, "What was the book?" Ross looks away slightly, then answers, "The Big Book of Children's Names."
Rachel turns away, putting the jar down on the counter.
Rachel turns back, placing a hand to her chest. "Okay, ross. Ross."
Rachel walks toward him, gesturing. "Okay, listen. What we have is amazing."
Rachel continues, her hands moving as she speaks. "But I do not want to have everything decided for me."
Rachel gestures. "I spent my whole life like that. It's what I had with Barry." Ross stands still, looking at her.
Rachel's voice rises slightly. "That was one of the reasons I left. I like not knowing right now."
Rachel gestures toward Ross. "And I'm sorry if that scares you. But if you want to be"
Rachel points a finger at Ross. "with me, you're going to have to deal with that." Ross nods and says, "Okay, fine." Rachel says, "Thank you." Ross points back, "We're not done."
Rachel touches her forehead, looking away. "I didn't know that."
Ross lowers his hand.
Ross steps forward and points at Rachel. "Then you're going to have to understand that you're with a guy who's not going to stop planning his future with you because he"
Ross keeps pointing. "knows we're going to end up together. And if that scares you, tough,"
Ross drops his hand. "because you're going to have to deal with that." Rachel stands with her hands on her hips. "Fine, I will." Ross replies, "Good."
Ross adds, "Because I love you." Rachel leans forward slightly. "Oh, yeah?"
Ross points a finger. "Well, I love you too." Rachel says, "Well, that's the first time we've said that." Ross replies, "Yes, it is." Rachel points at him. "Well, I'm going to kiss you."
Ross replies, "Well, you better."
Ross steps forward and kisses Rachel. They embrace. The setting changes to an exterior night view of a city skyline with lit skyscrapers and traffic below.

The setting changes to an apartment kitchen where Monica, wearing a white shirt and black pants, stands by the stove. Phoebe enters through the door, wearing a brown jacket over a dark turtleneck and carrying a backpack.
Phoebe says, "Hey."
Phoebe walks past the kitchen area. "Oh, thanks for the great movie tip," she says. Monica stirs a pot on the stove. "Yeah. Did you like?"
Phoebe responds, "Yeah. You know, I don't know if I was happier when"
Phoebe walks back toward the kitchen holding a VHS tape. "George Bailey destroyed the family business or Donna Reed cried"
Phoebe points with the tape. "or when the mean pharmacist made his ear bleed." Monica watches her, holding a spice jar.
Monica gestures with the spice jar.
Monica gestures. "All right, I'll give you the ear thing, but don't you think the ending was pretty wonderful?" Phoebe turns and walks away. "I didn't watch the ending. I was too depressed."
Phoebe walks toward the living area, gesturing. "It just kept getting worse and worse. It should have been called it's a sucky life."
Phoebe stops and turns back toward the kitchen. "And just when you think it can't suck anymore, it does."

Audience laughter is audible. The setting changes to an apartment where Joey, Chandler, and Richard are standing around a foosball table. Joey and Chandler grip the rods on one side; Richard stands on the other.
Joey pulls a rod. "Kick, save."
Chandler moves a rod. "And denied. He gets it back."
Chandler continues, "Pass to the middle, lines it up, and" Richard moves his rods quickly. The foosball makes a loud clack sound.
Richard stands up straight. Audience laughter is audible.
Richard says, "yes. Could that shot be any prettier?" Joey and Chandler look at him.
Joey gestures toward Richard.
Joey says, "Man, you are incredible."
Richard opens his arms. "Well, we had a table in college." Joey replies, "Oh, really?" Chandler scratches his head and says, "I didn't know they had foosball in the 18 hundreds."
Audience laughter is audible.
Richard points a finger at Chandler. "Nice mustache, by the way."
Richard continues, "When puberty hits, that thing's really going to kick in." Joey laughs.
Audience laughter is audible. Richard prepares to play again, gripping the foosball rods. Monica enters the room through a doorway.
Monica leans on the doorframe. "Honey, not to sound too Florence Henderson, but dinner's on the table."
Richard keeps his hands on the rods, looking at Monica. "Okay, just one more point."
Joey, Chandler, and Richard rapidly manipulate the foosball rods, the ball clattering loudly. A ball flies off the table and hits Monica. She winces, and audience laughter is audible.
Monica points a finger toward the table. "Now can we go?"
Chandler looks at Monica and points toward the table. "See, that's why we don't let her play."
Audience laughter is audible.
In an apartment kitchen area, Joey, Chandler, Monica, and Richard stand near a foosball table. Monica stands with her arms crossed, facing Joey and Chandler.
A voice asks, "Is everything all right?"
Joey and Chandler stand facing Monica and Richard. Chandler gestures with his hands.
Chandler says, "Oh, hey. Listen, don't be mad at him. It's our fault. I'm sorry we've been hogging so much of his time. Yeah, he's just really"
Joey adds, "great to hang out."
Joey gestures toward Richard and says, "No, no, seriously. Chandler and I were just talking about this. He is so much cooler than our"
Richard and Monica look at Joey, listening.
Joey continues, "I mean. You know, our dads are okay and all, but Richard is just" Chandler kicks Joey's leg. Joey looks at Chandler and asks, "what are you kicking me for?"
Chandler looks at Joey.
Chandler gestures with his thumb and says, "I'm trying to talk to him."
Richard and Monica look at Joey and Chandler. Joey points at Chandler.
Richard points to his chest and asks, "You guys see me as a dad?"
Joey says, "Oh, yeah." Chandler simultaneously says, "No," his pitch higher than Joey's.
Chandler shakes his head slightly at Joey. Joey looks at Chandler, his mouth slightly open.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "you're just clearly not familiar with our young person's vernacular. See, when we say dad, we mean buddy."
Richard smiles, looking at Chandler.
Chandler points to Joey and says, "We mean no, seriously. Joey's my dad."
Richard and Monica look at Chandler, their expressions neutral.
Chandler points to Monica and says, "Monica's my dad."
Chandler points to himself and says, "I've even got some dads down at work."
Richard chuckles and looks down briefly.
Richard puts his arm around Monica's shoulders and says, "That's fine. Well, your other dad and I are going to go have a romantic evening. And I guess I'll just see you kids around"
Richard and Monica turn and walk toward the door.
Chandler points toward the door and says, "99. You're not a dad."
Joey points toward the door and says, "You're not a dad."
Joey and Chandler lower their hands.
Chandler looks at Joey and says, "Can't believe you got us into trouble."
Chandler slaps Joey's chest. Joey slaps Chandler's arm. Chandler hits Joey's arm, and Joey hits Chandler back.
Chandler hits Joey again and runs toward the living room area. Joey chases him out of the frame.
The setting changes to the hallway outside the apartment. Richard and Monica walk out of the doorway and stop, facing each other.
Monica looks at Richard and asks, "It are you okay?"
Richard replies, "Yeah."
Richard puts his hands in his pockets.
Richard looks down slightly and says, "Just I feel like I'm about 100."
Monica looks at Richard.
Richard continues, "I thought I was just one of the guys."
Monica steps closer to Richard.
Monica extends her hand toward him and says, "Come here."
Monica says, "I'll make you feel like one of the guys." She touches his collar.
Monica wraps her arms around Richard's neck and kisses him. Richard holds her waist.
They pull back slightly, keeping their arms around each other.
Monica says, "For a really cool guy, you suck at foosball." Richard replies, "What are you talking about? I was killing them." Monica smiles and says, "Yeah, well, they suck too."
They kiss again. The setting changes to an apartment, where Ross and Rachel stand near a table with a baby lying on a changing pad.

Ross holds a white cloth and says, "Okay? And then you take the poopy diaper and you put it in the poopy diaper pail." Rachel secures the baby's diaper and says, "Okay, Ross, just so you know calling it a poopy diaper"
Rachel finishes, "doesn't make this process any cuter."
Ross turns and walks toward the apartment door as a buzz sounds.
Ross presses a button on the wall intercom and says, "Hello?"
A voice from the intercom says, "It's us." Ross replies, "Come on up. I'm going to get the rest of his stuff together." Rachel adds, "Okay."
Rachel finishes dressing the baby and says, "Okay. We can do this now, can't we, Ben?"
Rachel says, "Yes, we can."
Rachel continues securing the baby's clothes.
Rachel repeats, "Yes, we can."
Rachel leans back slightly from the table.
Rachel gestures with her hands toward the baby and says, "There, I did it."
Rachel raises her fists in a celebratory gesture, smiling widely at the baby.
Rachel says, "Look at that." She lifts the baby off the pad.
Rachel holds the baby up, supporting him under his arms.
Rachel says, "Stays on and everything."
Rachel smiles, looking at the baby.
The baby says, "Hi."
Rachel's eyes widen and her jaw drops as she stares at the baby.
Rachel maintains her surprised expression, holding the baby.
Rachel asks, "I'm sorry."
Rachel says, "What did you just say?"
Rachel says, "Just say hi."
Rachel holds the baby closer to her chest.
Rachel shouts loudly, "Oh, my God. Ross."
Rachel turns her head toward the hallway and shouts, "Ross. Ben just said hi. Ben just said hi."
Ross enters carrying a blue bag and asks, "What's the word?"
Ross asks, "Hi?" Rachel replies, "No. My uncle."
Rachel says, "Hi."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Great. And I miss that too. I miss everything," his volume rising on "miss everything." Rachel touches his arm and says, "Oh, I'm sorry."
Rachel says, "I guess I just bring it out in"
The baby looks toward Ross as Rachel holds him.
Inside an apartment, Rachel stands near Carol, who holds baby Ben, while Susan and Ross stand nearby. Overlapping excited greetings are audible.
Carol says, "hi."
Rachel touches her own head, looks at Carol, and says, "Guess what? Ben just said his first word."
Carol asks, "What did he say?"
Ross looks toward Carol and says, "Something about hi."
Carol smiles at the baby and says, "That's so exciting. Mommy is so proud of"
Carol and Rachel make high-pitched "hi" sounds toward the baby.
Rachel raises her index finger and says, "you know, actually, it's more,"
Rachel repeatedly says "hi" in a high-pitched, staccato voice, leaning toward the baby.
Susan points toward Rachel, then Carol and Ross join in making the same "hi" sounds at the baby.
Susan raises her hand and says, "uh oh, okay. This could go on for a while." Ross hands her a blue bag as she adds, "Got a cab waiting downstairs." Ross says, "Well, this was fun."
Ross steps close to the baby, touches his shoulder, and says, "We should really do it again sometime, Ben. What do you say?"
Ross says, "Okay."
Ross says, "All right."
Susan leans in and kisses Ross on the cheek.
Ross says, "Okay."
Susan waves and says, "Bye, you guys." Rachel says, "Take care." Ross says, "Bye, Ben."
The baby, Ben, says, "Bye."
Ross sharply turns his head, then fully turns toward the departing women, extending both hands forward.
Ross frantically points at the baby and says, "Did he just say he said bye. He said bye. He said bye to me," his volume rising.
The women turn back around in the doorway, smiling. Carol bounces the baby.
Ross presses both hands to his head and says, "I'm seeing him go off to college."
Susan gestures and says, "We got to go."
Susan says, "We've got that cab waiting."
Ross says, "Okay."
Susan says, "See you later." Ross says, "All right."
Susan says, "Bye." The women exit, and Rachel walks away from the door.
Ross says, "bye."
Ross closes the door, leans his back against it, and says, "Bye bye."
Ross places a hand on his chest, keeping his back against the door, and exhales deeply.
The setting changes to a living room. Phoebe sits on the floor holding Ben, pointing a remote at a television. The TV shows a puppet character in the sand.
The television plays, "Oh, wow. Look at this nice deep hole I've been digging. Hey, Bert, isn't this a nice hole here?" Phoebe lowers the remote and says, "Okay, Ben. This is the"
Phoebe looks at Ben and says, "part where Ernie buries Bert in the sand and can't find him."
Phoebe says, "Now, I've looked ahead on the tape, and he does find him again."
Phoebe continues looking at Ben, who chews on a toy.
Phoebe says, "But okay, before that happens there's some pretty rough going for a while. But I think we can handle it."
Phoebe says, "And there's just the alphabet, but we know that ends well."
Phoebe points the remote at the TV.
Phoebe says, "Okay, here we go," and presses a button on the remote.
The television shows the puppet character looking around.
The television plays, "What happened to my friend Bert?"
The television plays, "He was here just a moment ago. Oh, no. My old friend Bert is" as Phoebe pulls Ben closer and says, "I'm so glad you're here."
Phoebe holds the baby as he continues to chew on his toy.
The screen turns completely black and remains empty.
